name: src/events layout (V1)
description: Phase 6 module: tail.ts (EventBus pub/sub), poll.ts (SQLite-backed tailBurrow/tailAll), publish.ts (appendAndPublish helper), render.ts (NDJSON envelope + pretty), archive.ts (on-destroy NDJSON writer), destroy.ts (archive + prune + mark destroyed). Both bus and poll consume the same RunEvent shape from core/types.ts.

content: NDJSON envelope (SPEC §14.1) is the stable wire shape — keys: type='event', ts (ISO), burrowId, runId, seq, kind, stream, payload. Same envelope is reused by live tail render (renderNdjson) and the on-destroy archive (events.jsonl). Adding a new top-level key is breaking; adding a new kind value is additive. Use eventToEnvelope() in src/events/render.ts to construct it; do not hand-roll JSON.stringify with bespoke fields.

EventBus (src/events/tail.ts) is best-effort, in-process pub/sub only. SQLite events table is the source of truth (SPEC §14.3). The bus catches listener exceptions so one bad subscriber can't poison the rest, and silently no-ops after close(). Cross-process CLI tail uses tailBurrow/tailAll polling against SQLite — there is no IPC/daemon in V1.

name: append-and-publish-fanout
description: Helper appendAndPublish({repo, bus?, ...input}) in src/events/publish.ts inserts an event row (transactional, monotonic seq via EventsRepo.append) and then publishes the eventRowToEvent(row) result onto the bus. Use it from the run loop and runtime adapters so subscribers never lag behind storage. The bus is optional — the helper still works without one, so unit tests don't need to construct an EventBus just to write events.

name: poll-tail-with-cooperative-abort
description: tailBurrow/tailAll in src/events/poll.ts are async generators that loop {select past sinceSeq → yield → sleepOrAbort(intervalMs)} until once=true exhausts the source or signal aborts. sleepOrAbort returns true if the AbortSignal fires before the timer; the generator returns immediately, so callers (CLI commands, tests) can stop polling without leaked timers. Tests use pollIntervalMs=5 and abort the controller; production CLI defaults to 200ms.

content: EventsRepo.listByBurrow({limit}) returns the FIRST N events in seq-asc order — it caps from the beginning, not the end. For 'last N events' (live tail / dashboard event window), use EventsRepo.listTail(burrowId, limit) which queries seq-desc + limit and reverses the result so callers still get oldest-first within the window.
